CropLogic Ltd. ( (AU:OLY) ) has shared an announcement.

Olympio Metals shareholders approved all resolutions at a general meeting held in Perth, confirming broad support for the company’s recent and planned capital management activities. The passed resolutions ratify prior issues of placement shares, approve the issue of a second tranche of placement shares, and authorise new options to placement participants, corporate advisers and project partners, reinforcing the company’s funding structure and strategic project agreements.

The strong voting margins in favour of each resolution indicate investor backing for Olympio Metals’ current financing strategy and its associated exploration or development initiatives. These approvals provide the company with flexibility to proceed with its capital raising, incentive structures and share-based payments to counterparties such as Bullion Gold and Spark, which may underpin ongoing project advancement and advisory relationships.

More about CropLogic Ltd.

Olympio Metals Limited is an Australian-listed resources company focused on metals exploration and development. The company is listed on the ASX under the ticker OLY and operates from Perth, positioning itself within the broader mining and commodities sector.

data.title: Lupin’s U.S. Arm Settles Humana Antitrust Claim for $30 Million
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Lupin Limited ( (IN:LUPIN) ).

Lupin’s U.S. subsidiary, Lupin Pharmaceuticals, Inc., has been a defendant in consolidated civil antitrust litigation in Philadelphia over alleged anticompetitive conduct involving certain generic drug products. While the company maintains that the allegations are unfounded, it has opted to resolve one strand of the dispute with plaintiff Humana Inc. through a negotiated settlement.

Under the settlement, Lupin Pharmaceuticals will pay USD 30 million to Humana in exchange for a full and final release of all claims against the company and its affiliated entities, with the payment already provisioned in Lupin’s consolidated financial results. The agreement, which follows similar settlements by other defendants, allows Lupin to avoid further legal costs and uncertainty without admitting liability, limiting financial surprise for investors and providing greater clarity around one aspect of its U.S. legal exposures.

More about Lupin Limited

Lupin Limited is an India-headquartered global pharmaceutical company operating through subsidiaries such as Lupin Pharmaceuticals, Inc. in the U.S. It focuses on generic and specialty medicines, serving regulated markets including the United States, and is listed on both the National Stock Exchange of India and BSE Limited.

Average Trading Volume: 30,261

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: 1069.4B INR

See more data about LUPIN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

29metals Ltd. ( (AU:29M) ) just unveiled an announcement.

29Metals Limited has notified the market of the issuance of 18,222,692 unquoted performance rights under its employee incentive scheme. The new securities, designated as 29MAA performance rights and issued on 14 April 2026, are not intended to be quoted on the ASX, underscoring the company’s continued use of equity-linked rewards to incentivise staff and potentially influence future dilution and capital structure.

The issuance highlights 29Metals’ focus on long-term employee alignment with company performance, a common practice among ASX-listed resource companies seeking to retain key talent in a competitive market. While the performance rights do not immediately affect the company’s free float, they represent a contingent claim on future equity that existing shareholders and prospective investors may factor into assessments of ownership and dilution risk.

Ozz Resources Ltd ( (AU:OZZ) ) has provided an announcement.

Ozz Resources Ltd has applied to the ASX for quotation of 26,637,458 new ordinary fully paid shares, with an issue date of 17 April 2026. The securities form part of a previously announced transaction, and their quotation will expand the company’s listed share base, potentially enhancing liquidity for shareholders and supporting its ongoing corporate and project funding activities.

The application under Appendix 2A formalizes the transition of these issued securities to quoted status on the ASX, aligning them with existing ordinary shares on the market. data.title: China Resources Enterprise Exits Substantial Holding in Comvita
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Comvita ( (CVNZF) ) is now available.

China Resources Enterprise (CRE) has ceased to be a substantial shareholder in Comvita after selling its entire holding of 4,408,736 ordinary shares, previously representing about 6.25% of Comvita’s issued capital. The block was sold via a crossing at NZ$0.68 per share, for roughly NZ$3.0 million, removing a major institutional holder from the register and potentially altering Comvita’s shareholder base and trading dynamics, with settlement expected on 20 April 2026.

More about Comvita

Comvita Limited is a New Zealand-listed company, trading under ticker CVT, whose shares are held by a range of institutional investors, including international groups such as China Resources Enterprise. The company operates in the listed equity market, where changes in substantial shareholdings can influence liquidity, investor mix, and market perception of its stock.

The latest announcement is out from Haranga Resources Limited ( (AU:HAR) ).

Haranga Resources Limited has issued 500,000 fully paid ordinary shares following the exercise of an equivalent number of unlisted options at $0.08 each, with the new securities placed without a disclosure document under the relevant Corporations Act provisions. The company confirmed its ongoing compliance with financial reporting and continuous disclosure obligations, and noted that drilling is underway at its Lincoln Gold Project, with results to be analysed and released in line with its market reporting duties.

The share issue marginally increases Haranga’s capital base and reflects the conversion of options into equity, which may modestly strengthen its balance sheet and align option holders more closely with shareholders. Operationally, the reference to the active drilling program at Lincoln underscores continued investment in exploration, with upcoming assay results likely to influence market perceptions of the project’s potential and the company’s growth trajectory.

data.title: ImExHS Sets Hybrid AGM for May as It Formalises Digital Governance Approach
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from ImExHS Limited ( (AU:IME) ) is now available.

ImExHS Limited has called its Annual General Meeting for 11:00am AEST on 20 May 2026, to be held as a hybrid event at its Sydney CBD venue and via an online platform operated by Automic, allowing shareholders to attend, vote, and ask questions either in person or virtually. The company is moving away from routine hard-copy distribution of meeting documents in line with Corporations Act amendments, directing investors to access the Notice of Meeting and proxy materials online, while the board unanimously recommends voting in favour of all resolutions and stresses the importance of shareholder participation through poll-based voting.

Shareholders who wish to receive physical documentation can still request hard copies, but otherwise are expected to download the Notice of Meeting and instructions for electronic voting and question submission from the company’s website or ASX platform. By formalising digital access and an online participation structure, ImExHS is aligning its governance practices with updated Australian regulatory settings, potentially improving engagement and efficiency while reinforcing board support for the resolutions to be considered at the AGM.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:IME) stock is a Buy with a A$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ImExHS Limited stock, see the AU:IME Stock Forecast page.

More about ImExHS Limited

ImExHS Limited is an Australia-listed company that provides technology and services to healthcare providers, with a focus on medical imaging and related clinical workflows. Its shareholder base is primarily engaged through ASX disclosures and formal governance processes, including hybrid in-person and virtual meetings to facilitate participation across its investor community.

Average Trading Volume: 28,400

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$20.55M
